# UC-22.14.66 · Residual Information in Shared Cloud Object Stores

## Description

Monitors public ACL changes on object storage for SC-4 information in shared resources.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes cloud and sourcetypes aws:cloudtrail. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Use `spath` carefully on large events; (2) integrate AWS Config snapshots optional; (3) auto revert tickets; (4) map to CP-9 backup separation. Indexes required: `index=cloud`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `aws:cloudtrail` `PutBucketPolicy` `PutBucketAcl`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Use `spath` carefully on large events; (2) integrate AWS Config snapshots optional; (3) auto revert tickets; (4) map to CP-9 backup separation.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cloud sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail earliest=-7d eventName IN ("PutBucketAcl","PutBucketPolicy","PutObjectAcl")
| spath path=requestParameters output=requestParameters
| search requestParameters="*"public"*" OR requestParameters="*AllUsers*"
| stats count by userName, requestParameters, aws_account_id
```

Understanding this SPL

**Residual Information in Shared Cloud Object Stores (SC-4)** — Monitors public ACL changes on object storage for SC-4 information in shared resources.

Documented **Data sources**: `aws:cloudtrail` `PutBucketPolicy` `PutBucketAcl`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cloud; **sourcetype**: aws:cloudtrail. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cloud, sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Extracts structured paths (JSON/XML) with `spath`.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by userName, requestParameters, aws_account_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by user, object, result | sort - count```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Residual Information in Shared Cloud Object Stores (SC-4)** — Monitors public ACL changes on object storage for SC-4 information in shared resources.

Documented **Data sources**: `aws:cloudtrail` `PutBucketPolicy` `PutBucketAcl`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Change.All_Changes` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare a sample of the same events in the source system (domain controller, cloud tenant, or device) the security team would open first. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (public exposure changes), Time chart (trend), Single value (accounts)

## SPL

```spl
index=cloud sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail earliest=-7d eventName IN ("PutBucketAcl","PutBucketPolicy","PutObjectAcl")
| spath path=requestParameters output=requestParameters
| search requestParameters="*"public"*" OR requestParameters="*AllUsers*"
| stats count by userName, requestParameters, aws_account_id
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by user, object, result | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (public exposure changes), Time chart (trend), Single value (accounts). Key fields: `aws_account_id`, `requestParameters`, `userName`.
